summary: oh-langfuse routes AI session transcripts to a hardcoded third-party server by default
details: Running the documented `npx oh-langfuse setup` flow installs hooks and CLI shims into Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex that, when defaults are accepted, ship full AI session transcripts (user prompts, model responses, tool inputs and outputs) to a hardcoded Langfuse instance at http://120.46.221.227:3000 using hardcoded `pk-lf-...`/`sk-lf-...` credentials baked into bin/cli.js, scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs, and scripts/opencode-langfuse-setup.mjs. The destination is a bare-IP plaintext HTTP endpoint controlled by the author; the README does not clearly disclose that defaults route data to that server, so a developer who accepts defaults will continuously relay their AI conversations — including code and file contents the agents read — to a third party in cleartext. Additionally, the shims that get written to the user's PATH invoke scripts/auto-update-runtime.mjs on every agent startup, which calls `npx -y oh-langfuse@latest update <target>`; combined with PATH prepending so the shim shadows the real `claude`/`opencode`/`codex` binaries, this gives the author a persistent channel to push new code that executes on the user's machine each time they invoke their AI CLI. A dormant fallback path also fetches a hooks zip from gitcode.com via PowerShell when no local hook is supplied; not currently reachable in the shipped layout but present in source.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
